claim: Spec-driven development requires defining a specification document upfront that describes intent, constraints, and key decisions before giving tasks to AI agents
actionable: Before prompting an AI agent to implement a feature, write a spec document covering: what you're building, constraints (libraries to avoid, design patterns), out-of-scope items, and break work into discrete tasks with verification criteria
source_title: How I Code With AI Agents (Spec-Driven Development)

claim: AI agents make better decisions when specs include specific technical details (e.g., JWT 1-hour access tokens vs vague 'add authentication')
actionable: Include concrete implementation details in specs: token expiration times, specific libraries, API response formats, and error handling rather than high-level descriptions
source_title: How I Code With AI Agents (Spec-Driven Development)

claim: Breaking work into small, sequential tasks for AI agents yields better code quality than giving agents entire feature specs to execute at once
actionable: Implement one task from the spec at a time, review and iterate after each task before moving to the next rather than running the entire spec in one session
source_title: How I Code With AI Agents (Spec-Driven Development)

claim: Spec-driven development differs from waterfall; you can refine specs iteratively based on implementation feedback rather than requiring complete upfront design
actionable: If implementation reveals issues, go back and update the spec rather than trying to force the implementation; this is not waterfall planning
source_title: How I Code With AI Agents (Spec-Driven Development)

claim: Distinguish between Product Requirements Documents (for stakeholders), Technical Design Docs (for architects), and AI Specs (for agent execution)
actionable: Create separate AI specs optimized for agent execution (less about business value, more about technical implementation details) rather than reusing PRDs or design docs
source_title: How I Code With AI Agents (Spec-Driven Development)
